I'd like to make a statement today to avoid interacting with @/hannahbrrr.bsky.social as an art tracer.
The commission sketch I received was generative AI, and done so in a way to avoid being detected as such. 
This is not something I want to see from any artist in this space.

Is there confirmation that it was really ai generated? Hope it wasn’t but I wouldn’t be surprised. 

Generative ai “art” is evil.

I don’t want to read anything written by an LLM; I don’t want a machine to do any reading for me. I don’t want to watch movies or see visual art created by generative AI; I don’t want to financially support companies that replace human artists (or personal assistants!) with computers.

Gotta say, I’m impressed with Claude Cowork and Claude Code. I built a custom WP theme for my website (HTML/CSS.) A commissions ordering system for my art (PHP.) An automatic “AI” image content tagger (Python.) All within days. Generative “AI” is garbage. But assistive “AI” can be genuinely useful.

TIL Bitmoji has started using AI.

I may have to move away from Bitmoji.

To be clear, I'm not against AI -- it'd be silly to be blanket against AI in this industry. I am, however, opposed to /generative/ AI built on the backs of stolen work from human artists.

I wouldn't do it for a multitude of reasons but if someone did it I wouldn't immediately hate it.

Knock it the fuck off. Generative AI has no space in the creative world, be it art, writing, or other forms of media. To hide behind the trans community and use them as your excuse for why you used it is not only disgusting behavior, it's deplorable. We are not your skapegoat.

hand out flyers. The robot’s voice was monotone, repeating, "Generative AI is trash." I took a flyer, but it disintegrated in my hands. I would add that the reluctant consensus arrived at in this discussion - that AI can be art if the human is writing the prompt or manipulating it after - is wrong. It's not a tool, it's a service - you type in tags and then the machine correlates its hidden data that you don't have access to
Yeah, and even art theft aside, I fear that a whole generation of would-be creatives will have their creativity stifled by generative tech. The quality ceiling gets very low if it's all you know

The core idea is that religious traditions work like generative models. A low-d unified source (the Absolute) gets projected through tradition-specific "decoders" (Revelation) into the complex high-d forms we observe: doctrine, ritual, scripture, sacred art (the Exoteric)...

Generative AI is scrapers stealing and pulling millions of pieces of art, from wherever it can find it, mashing it all into a database, and then churning out (decreasingly, unfortunately) sloppy images that are amalgamations of pieces that fit a prompt, based on how it's broken down that stolen work

It still infuriates me how heavily they pushed the idea that "Generative ai" would grant accessibility when it did the opposite

It made many things less accessible, in addition to stealing from many artists who used art/writing as a form of income

I think it was good and vital this was brought up and aired out. The fandom has to make it clear the use of generative AI is entirely unacceptable. 

The artist had a chance to prove themselves and hopefully the art team understands not to dabble.

That said I think we need to keep an eye out.

Art must never be made by machines.

Assisted by them? Sure. There's plenty of tools that used proto-AI* technology to assist artists before the generative models even reared their head.

But there is no future in which AI can create our music, our movies and our games that ends well for us.
